attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who commits to long-range moves across multiple consecutive days, willing to spend significant energy in escape groups rather than waiting for decisive moments.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with significant autonomy in breakaways, though may lack team support in covering counter-moves when marking duties conflict with following key attacks.
terrain_preference: Grand Tour mountain stages where sustained breakaway efforts can yield GC gains; benefits from multi-day breakaway scenarios where accumulated time gains compound advantage.
vulnerability: Decision-making under pressure when multiple threats emerge simultaneously; tendency to fixate on marking specific riders at the expense of covering more dangerous moves.
key_patterns: Commits to multi-day breakaway efforts, maintaining form across consecutive aggressive stages (2 observations).
Shows tactical indecision when forced to choose between marking assignments versus covering attacks (2 observations).
Capable of significant GC gains through breakaway tactics rather than direct confrontation with rivals (2 observations).
form_trajectory: positive: 2019 Giro showed ability to gain substantial GC time through sustained breakaway success over multiple days
